item.title: "Daddy wait for me... I am going to make you coffee"
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: For Science!
item.description:
You might spot these CoastSnap points around Australia and the world. They make contributing to coastal research easy as 1, 2, 3! 1. Put your phone in the cradle 2. Snap a photo 3. Upload it to the CoastSnap app. CoastSnap relies on repeat photos at the same location to help researchers monitor how the coastlines are changing over time. It's just one way you can get involved in Citizen Science Month this April https://www.instagram.com/p/CcHUs_doch1/
Example
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: Socialism is scary
item.description: When I was living in Germany it was even worse, I just had to swipe my health insurance card and was never even charged as the insurance company pays them directly. *shudders*

item.dc:creator: MyUserNameDoesNotExist

item.description:
I'm going to stop for a bit.
I know this may seem silly...
But I'm a little hurt by the down votes on the post that criticizes white men...
I mean there's 50 images. Those are...3?
But I've never had a post go negative before.
I'm more sensitive than I thought!
Sorry!

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: I can explain!
item.description: Yes, it's a stolen image - and reposted - but that's not the point. I showed this to my wife and she just said "of course, morning people tend to be extroverts and want to be around others, so they want them to wake up and interact. Night people tend to be introverts and are afraid that they will wake someone up and have to talk to them." Makes perfect sense to me. For reference, me on left, her on right.

item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: This is my mom, me and my grandmother when we were homeless and living in a car
item.description:
This picture seems incredibly normal until I think about the time it was taken. This is relatively early into our homelessness, because my nana was still in the car with us. I was 17, and what we’d do is that we’d spend the afternoon at the beach nearby a bonfire. Thinking about it, it wasn’t so bad, that part. For a short while, it seemed my mother and grandmother got along for the first time in years.
This was the bonfire we were sitting by. I can still remember the cold air and the warm, crackly smell of the fire to this day, almost 5 years later. Edit: I have another post on my account, that shares backstory on my story of this experience. This is a minor part of that story, but I felt like sharing anyways. Full story here : https://imgur.com/gallery/WxhPKpd
item.dc:creator: personalrandomgirlaccount

item.dc:creator: MovieCaveDave

item.title: Well shit
item.description: My wife of 5 years, who I have a 3 year old with, and I have had major issues due to her mental health for years. I’ve been so warn down by her drinking and anger that I’ve finally decided enough is enough. I had plans of asking her to separate and eventually divorce this weekend after yet another horrible week with her. I support the house fully while she decided to go back to school. I got a phone call yesterday from her crying her eyes out that her mother had finally not come back from another OD. there’s a ton of back story that I won’t get into but my wife’s child hood was an absolute nightmare due to her drug addict parents. Obviously this had caused her some major lasting damage. I’ve tried so hard to help and be supportive but there’s a limit to what a person can take. I finally hit that point and now this happens. I’m just tired. I feel thin. I have to be the supportive one because there really isn’t a choice. I can’t be the worst person in the world and leave her while this is going on. I can’t do that but now I’m left to make all the arrangements and cover the cost of whatever needs to be done which I don’t have the money to do. I have no idea how I’m going to get through this in one piece. I feel selfish and angry and sad. I’ve already been sucking it up for our sons sake and trying not to be an asshole to someone who walks all over me and treats everyone around her like garbage. I thought I was going to loose my mind before this shit. Don’t do drugs people. The effects are far reaching and extremely destructive. Sorry just venting to the void. Don’t really have anyone to talk to.
item.dc:creator: therobotmd101
